title: BCM/FMC Talks Conclude in Quanzhou with 'Initial Exchanges' on Oil and Gas Cooperation
detail: Philippines-China BCM and FMC talks concluded in Quanzhou on March 28. Both sides agreed on 'initial exchanges on potential oil and gas cooperation,' discussed coast guard-to-coast guard communication frameworks, ocean meteorology, green energy, trade, agriculture, and fertilizer supply chains. Both delegations agreed to convene a foreign ministers' meeting 'within the year.' China's Vice Foreign Minister Sun Weidong called for 'concrete actions' from Manila to translate goodwill into results. The DFA (in its March 29 readout) described the talks as a 'frank and thorough exchange.' US News/Reuters reported that 'Manila and Beijing resumed talks on South China Sea and energy security' — the first substantive bilateral consultations on the disputed waters in over two years.

title: China Issues Counter-Statement Blaming Philippines for BRP Benguet Near-Collision
detail: China's People's Liberation Army issued an official counter-statement on March 28 regarding the March 25 near-collision between BRP Benguet and PLAN frigate 532 near Pag-asa Island. Beijing claimed a PLA Navy ship was conducting a 'routine patrol near Zhubi Reef' (Subi Reef) when a Philippine vessel 'ignored repeated radio warnings and altered course at close range,' characterizing Manila's actions as 'dangerous and unprofessional.' The statement inverted the Philippine AFP's account, in which PLAN frigate 532 was accused of making an intentional unsafe maneuver against BRP Benguet. Both the AFP and the PLA published versions of the incident, with no independent verification available. The Philippines' Western Command (WESCOM) confirmed BRP Benguet's account was accurate and said China's statement was 'self-serving and false.'
